title: The Most Powerful Engine on Earth

beats.narration: From orbit, it looks almost gentle. It is the most powerful engine on Earth — and it builds itself.
beats.understand: open on the storm with real motion and a promise (it BUILDS ITSELF) — a hook, not a static postcard

footage.intent: a hurricane spiral from the ISS, pushing in fast so the frame is alive from the first second

beats.narration: It starts with warm ocean — water above twenty-six degrees, pouring heat and moisture into the sky.
beats.understand: the one ingredient — warm sea — landed fast

footage.intent: warm tropical ocean under broken cloud

beats.narration: That warm, wet air rushes upward. It cools, it rains, and every drop releases heat that pulls up even more. The storm becomes its own engine, feeding on the sea.
beats.understand: the self-feeding heat-engine loop, shown as a LIVING animated diagram (constant motion, non-cloud variety) instead of a held cloud plate

beats.narration: We rank them by their winds — from a category one, to a five past two hundred and fifty, strong enough to erase a coastline.
beats.understand: a brief, bold wind-scale check landing the top-of-scale number

beats.narration: And at its heart, the eye. Dead calm, ringed by the strongest winds on the planet.
beats.understand: the showpiece — the eye — on the most dynamic storm window, pushed in fast so it lands as a reveal

footage.intent: the churning heart of the storm from orbit, a fast push into the eye — the peak

beats.narration: Warm water and air. That is all it is. And once it begins to turn, nothing on Earth can stop it.
beats.understand: the thesis, ESCALATING — pull OUT to reveal the full storm (open up, don't loop back to the static opening frame)
beats.text: Nothing on Earth can stop it.

footage.intent: pull back from the storm to reveal its full scale as the thesis lands — an ending that opens up, not a return to the opening
